title: Barcoding site-saturation mutagenesis libraries
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tfpm","text":"This protocol details the barcoding of site-saturation mutagenesis library plasmids (generated in the last protocol) to enable high-throughput functional analysis of genetic variants. A custom barcode oligonucleotide is generated through annealing and polymerase extension on an ultramer ordered through Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT), then inserted into the library plasmid. This barcoding approach facilitates the tracking and analysis of numerous genetic variants within mutagenesis libraries, providing a systematic method to evaluate the functional impact of mutations. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Digest 1 µg of each sublibrary plasmid at 37 °C for 05:00:00 with NheI-HF and SacI-HF, reaction setup as below: %s %s
Clean up the gel-extracted sublibrary with Zymo Clean and Concentrator-5, eluting in 10 µL nuclease-free water.
Cut the digested plasmid out of the gel and extract the product using QIAquick Gel Extraction Kit according to the manufacturer’s protocol.
Run digestions on 1% agarose gel (made with 1X SYBR Safe) for 00:45:00 at 100V.
After 5 hours, add 1 µL rSAP to each reaction, then incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 , followed by heat inactivation at 65 °C for 00:20:00 .
Resuspend IDT Ultramer (NP0490) at 10uM.
Anneal NP0490 with primers in a reaction as outlined below: %s %s
Run the reaction in a thermocycler with the following program: 98 °C for 00:03:00 , followed by ramping down to 25 °C at -0.1C per second.
After annealing, add the following to each reaction: %s %s
Run the reaction in a thermocycler with the following program: %s %s
Once at 37 °C , add 1 µL each of NheI-HF and SacI-HF to each reaction (Total volume is now 44.15 µL ).
Digest the reaction at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
Run digested product on a 4% agarose gel (made with 1X SYBR Safe) for 00:45:00 at 100V.
Cut out the band and extract the product with QIAquick Gel Extraction kit, following manufacturer’s protocol.
Clean up the gel-extracted barcode oligonucleotide with Zymo Clean and Concentrator-5 kit, eluting in 30 µL nuclease-free water.
Prepare ligation reaction containing a 7:1 molar ratio of barcode oligonucleotide to sublibrary, as follows: %s %s
Ligate Overnight at 16 °C .
Clean ligation product with Zymo Clean and Concentrator-5, eluting in 10 µL nuclease-free water.
Pre-chill electroporation cuvettes (2 replicates for each sublibrary product, 1 positive control, 1 negative control) On ice for at least 00:30:00 .

During incubation, prepare 4 growth flasks for each sample. There will be 4 dilutions of the recovery volume for each sample: 500 µL , 250 µL , 125 µL , and 50 µL . Add 49 mL LB and 50 µL of carbenicillin to each flask.
Recover the cells in a shaking incubator ( 225 rpm, °C, ) at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
Recover each sample with 100 µL pre-warmed SOC, then add all 125 µL to the appropriate recovery tube – combine replicate electroporations. Total volume in the recovery tube should be 1 mL .
Electroporate each cuvette at 6 kV for 2 ms.
Wipe each cuvette down with a kimwipe to ensure no water or ice is stuck to the cuvette. Gently tap on the table top to ensure all liquid is collected at the bottom of the cuvette.

During incubation: prepare recovery tubes. For each sublibrary: add 750 µL SOC into 1 recovery tube (both electroporations will be combined) For each control: add 875 µL SOC into 1 recovery tube. Pre-warm the recovery tubes at 37 °C .
Allow the cells and plasmids to incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .

Add 1 µL of cleaned Gibson-assembled product into each of the replicate cuvettes. Add 1 µL pUC19 to the positive control and 1 µL of negative control from the Gibson assembly to the negative control cuvette. Take care not to introduce bubbles to the cuvette.
Pipette 25 µL NEB-10beta electrocompetent cells into each pre-chilled cuvette. Take care not to introduce bubbles to the cuvette.
Thaw an appropriate volume of NEB 10-beta electrocompetent cells On ice for 00:10:00 .
After recovery incubation, add the corresponding volume to the prepared growth flask. Incubate with shaking for 00:03:00 .
While the cultures are spinning, count colonies on the serial dilution plates. Estimate the number of barcoded variants present in each sublibrary for each bottleneck.
After 16 hours, remove the flasks from the incubator. Transfer the culture to a 50-mL falcon tube and spin for 4000 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes .
Serial dilution plates should be incubated for 16:00:00 at 37 °C in a standing incubator.

After 3 minutes, take a 200 µL sample from each flask to use for estimating colony counts via serial dilutions on LB-amp plates. Return the growth flasks to the incubator and incubate with shaking ( 225 rpm, °C, ) for 16:00:00 at 37 °C .
Use the Qiagen Plasmid Plus midiprep kit according to the manufacturer’s protocol to isolate plasmid DNA from the culture pellets.
creator: Sarah Heidl
